duplicate_groups.theme: Mobile login fails after password reset

issues.summary: Users unable to login to mobile banking app after password reset
Mobile app login fails after changing password
Authentication error when accessing mobile banking post password update

duplicate_groups.similarity_explanation: All three issues describe the identical problem: users cannot log into the mobile banking app after resetting/changing their password. SCRUM-1 reports the symptom (Invalid credentials error, 150 users/day). SCRUM-2 adds the error code AUTH-401, complaint volume (200/week), and the 15-20 minute workaround delay. SCRUM-3 identifies the root cause — stale session tokens and cache synchronization between the auth service and mobile API gateway. Together they paint a complete picture of one bug.
duplicate_groups.recommended_action: Keep SCRUM-3 as the primary issue since it identifies the root cause (cache sync issue). Link SCRUM-1 and SCRUM-2 as duplicates. Merge the quantitative impact data (150 users/day, 200 complaints/week, AUTH-401 error code) into SCRUM-3's description.

solution.description: Fix the cache synchronization between the authentication service and the mobile API gateway so that new credentials are recognized immediately after a password reset.
solution.technical_details: 1. Investigate the caching layer between the auth service and mobile API gateway — likely a Redis or Memcached instance holding stale credential hashes. 2. On password reset, issue a cache invalidation event (pub/sub or direct purge) for the affected user's credential entry. 3. Ensure the old session token is explicitly revoked upon password change (add to a token blacklist or revoke in the OAuth provider). 4. Reduce or eliminate the credential cache TTL for recently-reset passwords. 5. Add a cache-aside pattern where the gateway falls through to the auth service DB on AUTH-401 for recently changed accounts. 6. Verify fix on both iOS and Android. The 15-20 minute workaround delay likely corresponds to the cache TTL — confirm and reduce.

duplicate_groups.theme: Wire transfer timeout under heavy load

issues.summary: Wire transfer timeout errors during peak hours
SWIFT payment processing timeout under heavy load
International wire transfers failing with timeout at high traffic

duplicate_groups.similarity_explanation: All three describe wire transfer timeouts under high load. SCRUM-4 covers both domestic and international wires timing out at peak hours (504 after 30s, traced to payment orchestration service v3.2.1). SCRUM-5 specifically focuses on SWIFT international transfers with connection pool exhaustion on the SWIFT gateway adapter. SCRUM-6 describes international wire timeouts correlated with US-EU market overlap and a broken retry mechanism. While SCRUM-5 and SCRUM-6 are narrower (international only), the underlying root cause — the payment orchestration/gateway infrastructure cannot handle peak load — is the same, and the fix will address all three.
duplicate_groups.recommended_action: Keep SCRUM-4 as the primary (broadest scope covering domestic + international). Link SCRUM-5 and SCRUM-6 as duplicates. Consolidate the SWIFT connection pool exhaustion detail from SCRUM-5 and the broken retry mechanism from SCRUM-6 into SCRUM-4.

solution.description: Resolve connection pool exhaustion and timeout failures in the payment orchestration and SWIFT gateway services under peak load.
solution.technical_details: 1. Increase the SWIFT gateway adapter connection pool size and tune pool settings (max idle, connection timeout, eviction policy). 2. Investigate the payment orchestration service v3.2.1 deployment — diff against v3.2.0 for regressions (likely a connection handling or thread pool change). 3. Fix the broken retry mechanism: implement exponential backoff with jitter and circuit breaker pattern for downstream SWIFT calls. 4. Increase the HTTP timeout from 30s to an appropriate value or implement async processing with a callback/webhook for long-running transfers. 5. Add horizontal scaling (auto-scale pods) for the payment orchestration service during peak hours (10am-2pm). 6. Implement a queue-based architecture to decouple request acceptance from SWIFT processing, returning an immediate 202 Accepted with status polling. 7. Add monitoring/alerting for connection pool utilization and queue depth.

issues.summary: AML report generation takes over 30 minutes
Anti-money laundering daily reports are extremely slow

duplicate_groups.similarity_explanation: All three describe the same performance issue with compliance report generation. SCRUM-7 and SCRUM-8 both specifically call out AML reports taking 25-40 minutes with full table scans instead of indexed queries. SCRUM-9 is slightly broader (AML, CTR, and SAR reports) and attributes the degradation to transaction volume growth from 3 new institutional clients. The root cause is the same: the report query engine is not using proper indexes on a growing transaction dataset.
duplicate_groups.recommended_action: Keep SCRUM-9 as the primary since it has the broadest scope (all compliance reports, not just AML). Link SCRUM-7 and SCRUM-8 as duplicates. Merge the specific details about full table scans and missing date-partitioned indexes from SCRUM-7/SCRUM-8 into SCRUM-9.

solution.description: Optimize compliance report queries to use proper indexing and partitioning on the transactions database to bring generation time under the 5-minute regulatory SLA.
solution.technical_details: 1. Add or rebuild date-partitioned indexes on the transaction ledger (partition by transaction_date). 2. Rewrite report queries to leverage partition pruning — ensure WHERE clauses filter on the partition key. 3. Add composite indexes for the most common report filters (account_type, transaction_amount, risk_score, date). 4. Consider materialized views or pre-aggregation tables that refresh incrementally for standard report patterns. 5. Implement query result caching for reports run multiple times with the same parameters. 6. For the increased data volume from new institutional clients, evaluate table partitioning strategy (monthly vs. weekly partitions). 7. Add query execution plan monitoring to detect future full-table-scan regressions. 8. As a short-term fix, schedule reports to pre-generate 5 minutes before market close using the nearly-final dataset.

issues.summary: Third-party payment gateway returning 503 errors intermittently
Payment gateway integration sporadic service unavailable errors

duplicate_groups.similarity_explanation: Both issues describe the same problem: intermittent 503 Service Unavailable errors from the payment gateway integration affecting 2-4% of card transactions with no discernible pattern. SCRUM-10 adds that the gateway vendor blames malformed headers on our side. SCRUM-11 notes the impact on POS debit card purchases. They are clearly the same bug.
duplicate_groups.recommended_action: Keep SCRUM-10 as the primary (it includes the vendor's feedback about malformed headers, which is a key diagnostic clue). Link SCRUM-11 as duplicate. Add the POS/debit card context from SCRUM-11 to SCRUM-10.

solution.description: Diagnose and fix the malformed API request headers causing intermittent 503 errors from the third-party payment gateway (Stripe/Adyen).
solution.technical_details: 1. Capture and log full HTTP request headers for both successful and failed (503) gateway calls to identify the malformed header. 2. Compare request headers against the gateway's API specification — check Content-Type, Authorization, Idempotency-Key, and any custom headers. 3. Check for race conditions in the API client library that could corrupt headers under concurrent access (thread safety of HTTP client instances). 4. Verify that connection reuse (HTTP keep-alive) is not causing stale auth tokens in headers. 5. Implement request/response correlation logging with the gateway's transaction IDs for cross-referencing with their support. 6. Add retry logic with fresh headers for 503 responses (with idempotency keys to prevent double-charging). 7. Set up real-time alerting when the 503 error rate exceeds 1%.

duplicate_groups.theme: Dashboard balance not updating in real-time

issues.summary: Account balance not refreshing in real-time on dashboard
Dashboard shows stale balance data - not updating automatically

duplicate_groups.similarity_explanation: Both describe the customer dashboard failing to show updated account balances after transactions. SCRUM-12 points to WebSocket connections dropping silently. SCRUM-13 points to a broken polling mechanism not fetching from the account service API. They may reflect two facets of the same real-time update failure (WebSocket primary mechanism failing, and the polling fallback also broken), or they could be the same symptom observed from different debugging angles. Either way, the user-facing problem is identical.
duplicate_groups.recommended_action: Keep SCRUM-12 as the primary since it identifies the WebSocket issue. Link SCRUM-13 as duplicate. Include the polling fallback failure details from SCRUM-13 in SCRUM-12's description.

solution.description: Fix both the WebSocket real-time connection and the polling fallback to ensure account balances update automatically on the dashboard.
solution.technical_details: 1. Investigate WebSocket disconnections: check load balancer WebSocket support (upgrade headers, idle timeout settings), proxy configurations, and server-side heartbeat/ping-pong frames. 2. Add WebSocket reconnection logic with exponential backoff on the frontend. 3. Fix the polling fallback: ensure the frontend falls back to periodic API polling (e.g., every 10-15 seconds) when the WebSocket connection is unavailable. 4. Verify the account service API endpoint returns fresh data (check for caching headers, CDN caching, or stale read replicas). 5. Add a visible indicator on the dashboard showing last-updated timestamp and connection status. 6. Implement server-sent events (SSE) as a middle-ground fallback between WebSocket and polling. 7. Add monitoring for WebSocket connection drop rates and polling failure rates.

issues.summary: Implement real-time fraud detection for contactless card payments
Add ML-based fraud scoring for tap-to-pay transactions

duplicate_groups.similarity_explanation: Both stories request ML-based real-time fraud detection for contactless/tap-to-pay transactions. SCRUM-19 frames it as moving from batch to real-time with sub-second scoring and integration with the existing rules engine. SCRUM-20 focuses on reducing the 12% false positive rate with an ML model under 200ms latency. They describe the same feature initiative — the scope, technology (ML scoring), transaction type (contactless/NFC), and latency requirements overlap almost entirely.
duplicate_groups.recommended_action: Keep SCRUM-19 as the primary (broader scope including rules engine integration). Link SCRUM-20 as duplicate. Add the specific false-positive rate (12%) and 200ms latency requirement from SCRUM-20 into SCRUM-19's acceptance criteria.

solution.description: Implement a real-time ML-based fraud scoring system for contactless card payments, integrated with the existing rules engine, targeting sub-200ms latency and significant false-positive reduction.
solution.technical_details: 1. Train an ML model (gradient boosting or neural network) on historical contactless transaction data — features: amount, merchant category, location, time, velocity, device fingerprint. 2. Deploy the model as a low-latency inference service (e.g., TensorFlow Serving, ONNX Runtime) behind the existing rules engine. 3. Implement a hybrid scoring pipeline: rule-based pre-filters → ML model scoring → combined risk score. 4. Target <200ms end-to-end latency using model optimization (quantization, feature store with pre-computed features). 5. Set up A/B testing framework to compare ML model vs. current rule-based system on false positive rate and fraud catch rate. 6. Implement model monitoring for drift detection and automated retraining pipeline. 7. Add a feedback loop from fraud investigations to improve model accuracy over time.

issues.summary: Loan approval workflow stuck in pending state for 48+ hours
Mortgage application processing pipeline hangs indefinitely

duplicate_groups.similarity_explanation: Both describe loan/mortgage application workflows getting stuck and not progressing. SCRUM-21 is about the automated decision engine not triggering the next step after credit checks (personal loans, auto loans, credit lines — 300+ stuck). SCRUM-22 is about mortgage applications hanging after document verification due to a dead letter queue issue in the message broker. While they affect different loan types and different workflow stages, they share the same pattern: a message/event-driven workflow pipeline failing to advance. The root cause may differ (decision engine trigger vs. dead letter queue), so confidence is MEDIUM.
duplicate_groups.recommended_action: Keep both issues but link them as related. SCRUM-21 covers general loan workflow stalling (decision engine trigger failure), SCRUM-22 covers mortgage-specific pipeline hang (dead letter queue). If investigation reveals a common message broker issue, merge into one. Otherwise, treat as related but distinct bugs that should be fixed in the same sprint.

solution.description: Fix the workflow orchestration failures in both the loan approval decision engine and the mortgage document-to-underwriting message pipeline.
solution.technical_details: 1. For SCRUM-21: Investigate the automated decision engine's event listener — check if the credit check completion event is being published correctly and if the decision engine's consumer is subscribed and healthy. Check for stuck message acknowledgments or consumer group lag. 2. For SCRUM-22: Inspect the dead letter queue between document service and underwriting service — process or replay the dead-lettered messages, fix the deserialization or validation error causing messages to be DLQ'd. 3. For both: Add workflow state monitoring with alerts when any application stays in a single state beyond a configurable threshold (e.g., 4 hours). 4. Implement a compensation/retry mechanism that automatically retries stuck transitions. 5. Add a manual override dashboard for operations staff to view stuck workflows and trigger re-processing. 6. Audit the message broker (Kafka/RabbitMQ) health: check partition assignments, consumer lag, and DLQ depth.

issues.summary: User sessions expiring prematurely during online banking
Customers getting logged out randomly while using internet banking

duplicate_groups.similarity_explanation: Both describe customers being unexpectedly logged out during active online banking sessions. SCRUM-23 identifies the symptom (2-3 min expiry instead of 15 min, 20% of sessions affected) and points to session cookies losing their refresh token. SCRUM-24 identifies the root cause as load balancer session affinity — when traffic is routed to a different backend server, the session is not recognized. These are the same bug: sticky sessions failing at the load balancer cause the session/refresh token to be unrecognized, resulting in premature logout.
duplicate_groups.recommended_action: Keep SCRUM-24 as the primary since it identifies the root cause (load balancer session affinity). Link SCRUM-23 as duplicate. Add the quantitative data from SCRUM-23 (20% of sessions, 2-3 min vs. 15 min configured timeout) to SCRUM-24.

solution.description: Fix load balancer session affinity and implement server-independent session management to eliminate premature logouts.
solution.technical_details: 1. Move session state from local server memory to a centralized session store (Redis/Memcached) so any backend server can validate any session. 2. Alternatively, fix load balancer sticky session configuration — ensure cookie-based affinity is properly configured and the session affinity cookie is not being dropped. 3. If using JWT tokens, ensure the refresh token is validated against a central store, not server-local state. 4. Check load balancer health checks — unhealthy backend servers being removed from the pool will break affinity for their sessions. 5. Verify that the session cookie's domain, path, SameSite, and Secure attributes are correct and consistent across all backend servers. 6. Add session continuity monitoring: alert when the premature logout rate exceeds a threshold. 7. Test with simulated load balancer failover to verify sessions survive backend server changes.

unique_issues.summary: Credit score calculation module needs FICO Score 10 update

solution.description: Upgrade the credit scoring engine from FICO Score 8 to FICO Score 10. Update the scoring algorithm, recalibrate risk thresholds and underwriting decision boundaries, run parallel scoring (v8 and v10) during a transition period to validate, and retrain the underwriting team on new score ranges and interpretations.

unique_issues.summary: Database connection pool exhaustion during batch processing

solution.description: Fix connection leak in nightly batch jobs and isolate batch processing from online banking's connection pool. Implement proper try-with-resources / connection release in batch job code, increase pool size or use a dedicated pool for batch, and add connection pool utilization monitoring with alerts.

unique_issues.summary: Missing audit trail for admin privilege escalation

solution.description: Implement comprehensive audit logging for all administrative privilege changes. Log timestamp, user ID, target privileges, justification, and approval chain to a tamper-proof audit store. Add a mandatory approval workflow for privilege escalation. This is a SOX compliance critical finding requiring immediate action.

unique_issues.summary: KYC document upload fails for files larger than 5MB

solution.description: Increase the file size limit to at least 15MB for KYC uploads, add proper client-side and server-side file size validation with clear error messages, and implement client-side image compression to reduce file sizes before upload. Fix the API to return 413 Payload Too Large instead of generic 500.

unique_issues.summary: Two-factor authentication SMS delivery delayed by 10+ minutes

solution.description: Switch to a multi-channel OTP delivery approach: add push notification and authenticator app (TOTP) options as primary 2FA, keep SMS as fallback. Investigate carrier-level SMS delays with the SMS provider, consider switching providers or adding a second provider for failover. Extend OTP expiry to 10 minutes as a short-term fix.

unique_issues.summary: Migrate legacy COBOL batch jobs to microservices architecture

solution.description: Phase the migration using the strangler fig pattern: identify batch jobs, decompose into bounded contexts (reconciliation, interest posting, statements), build event-driven microservices in parallel, run dual-mode (COBOL + microservice) with output comparison, and cut over incrementally. Ensure ACID compliance using the saga pattern with compensating transactions.

unique_issues.summary: Enable recurring payment setup from mobile banking app

solution.description: Extend the existing recurring payments API (currently used by web portal) to the mobile app. Build mobile UI for schedule configuration (frequency, start/end date, amount). Reuse backend logic and add push notification reminders before each scheduled payment execution.

unique_issues.summary: Currency conversion rates not updating for forex transactions

solution.description: Fix the stale FX rate feed by restarting and monitoring the rate refresh cron job. Add health checks and alerting for FX feed freshness (alert if rates are >5 minutes old). Implement a circuit breaker that blocks forex transactions when rates are stale beyond a threshold. Add a fallback FX feed provider.

unique_issues.summary: Implement PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication compliance

solution.description: Implement SCA across all payment flows: add dynamic linking (transaction amount + payee in auth challenge), 90-day re-authentication for account access, and proper SCA challenge delegation for third-party payment initiators. Integrate with existing biometric and OTP infrastructure. Must be completed before Q3 audit deadline.

unique_issues.summary: Account statement PDF generation produces corrupted files

solution.description: Fix the PDF rendering engine's memory overflow for large statements (500+ transactions). Implement paginated/chunked PDF generation that processes transactions in batches. Switch to a streaming PDF library if the current one buffers everything in memory. Add output validation that verifies PDF integrity before serving to customers.

unique_issues.summary: Integrate Open Banking API for third-party fintech partners

solution.description: Build PSD2/Open Banking-compliant API endpoints for AIS and PIS. Implement OAuth 2.0 consent management with granular scopes, build a developer portal with sandbox environment, and ensure all endpoints meet Open Banking UK and Berlin Group standards. Include rate limiting, audit logging, and consent dashboard for customers.

unique_issues.summary: Scheduled maintenance window notifications not sent to customers

solution.description: Fix the notification scheduler service's silent failure — add proper error handling, logging, and retry logic. Implement a verification step that confirms notifications were queued and delivered before proceeding with maintenance. Add a pre-maintenance checklist that includes notification delivery confirmation as a gate.

unique_issues.summary: Upgrade TLS certificates to TLS 1.3 across all banking endpoints

solution.description: Plan a phased TLS 1.3 rollout: inventory all endpoints and third-party integrations, verify TLS 1.3 compatibility with each partner, upgrade load balancers and API gateways first, then internal service mesh. Maintain TLS 1.2 fallback during transition. Coordinate with payment processors and credit bureaus for compatibility testing.

unique_issues.summary: Duplicate transaction entries appearing in account history

solution.description: Fix the presentation layer bug in the account history API that shows duplicate transactions. Investigate whether the API is joining multiple data sources (e.g., pending + posted) and deduplicating incorrectly, or if a caching layer is serving stale + fresh data combined. Add unique transaction ID-based deduplication at the API response level.

unique_issues.summary: Enable biometric authentication for high-value transfers

solution.description: Extend the existing biometric auth module (currently login-only) to support transaction authorization. Implement PSD2-compliant dynamic linking by including transaction amount and payee in the biometric challenge. Support Face ID and fingerprint for transfers >1,000 EUR, beneficiary additions, and standing order changes.

unique_issues.summary: Interest calculation rounding errors on savings accounts

solution.description: Replace floating-point arithmetic with fixed-point decimal (e.g., BigDecimal or integer-based cent calculations) in the compound interest formula. Implement banker's rounding (round half to even). Run a reconciliation to calculate and correct cumulative discrepancies across all 2 million accounts. Add automated rounding validation tests.

unique_issues.summary: Implement data masking for PII in non-production environments

solution.description: Implement a data masking pipeline that runs during database refresh to non-prod environments. Use format-preserving encryption or tokenization for SSNs, account numbers, names, addresses, and phone numbers. Ensure masked data maintains referential integrity and realistic format for testing. Automate as part of the environment refresh process.

unique_issues.summary: Mobile check deposit image processing fails on Android 14

solution.description: Update the OpenCV image processing library to a version compatible with Android 14's new camera API (Camera2/CameraX). If OpenCV doesn't yet support Android 14, use Android's native CameraX API as an interim solution for image capture, passing the result to OpenCV for processing. Test across multiple Android 14 devices from different manufacturers.

unique_issues.summary: Add real-time push notifications for all account activities

solution.description: Build an event-driven notification service that subscribes to all account activity events (deposits, withdrawals, card transactions, logins, threshold breaches). Implement configurable notification preferences per account and per channel (push, SMS, email). Use a message broker to decouple event production from notification delivery. Include a preferences UI in both mobile and web.
